filename: Testing the effect of freezing of IF slides.docx
relative_path: tCycIF projects/Instructions/Testing the effect of freezing of IF slides.docx
full_text:
Testing the effect of freezing IF slides on IF signal and tissue morphology

AIM:

The fluorescence signal of tCycIF slides decreases over time when kept in PBS in the fridge. Currently, we can keep the slides in the fridge for around 1 month between/after the staining.

The aim is to test whether we could extend the time during which the slides remain usable by freezing them. After freezing we want to test the applications in 1) Cycif 2) FISH.

THINGS TO BE DECIDED/CHECKED BEFORE STARTING:

Sample selection? (Hanna on Friday)

3 different samples with different sizes (small, medium, big), 3 (optimally consecutive) sections from each sample

The easiest is to select already cut sections from the cold room

Starting time? (Hanna and Foteini will adjust after Pablo’s schedule has been confirmed)

Most convenient would be to do the pre-treatment (deparaff., pre-stain, bleach, stain) at the same time with Pablo’s tCycIF project

Checking the availability of glycerol → we will use 30% solution in PBS (RNase- and nuclease-free) (Foteini will check)

Making the automated tCycIF sheet (can be done after confirming the samples and markers) (Hanna on Friday)

CANDIDATE SAMPLES:

Also 21_KRAS project slides in Box_AI_p21_1, sectioned 3/2026 could be used. Only 3 sections left

H&Es: P:\h30492\farkkilab2\21_KRAS\H&E

quite a lot of stroma and some adipose but small and good quality section

QUESTIONS AND READOUTS:

Tissue morphology and integrity

Does freezing cause morphological changes in cell nuclei or cytoplasm?

visual comparison with pre- and post-freeze scans for respective cells (tumor, stroma, immune)

Does freezing lead to tissue detachment from slides?

visual comparison between pre- and post-freeze

annotating intact/detached areas with Qupath to help comparison and the change of detachment

Does freezing lead to cracking or other structural damage in tissue sections?

annotating folds and cracks with Qupath to help identifying and comparing the change between freezing cycles

IF signal

IF staining performance

Does freezing affect IF signal intensity?

Checking min/max intensities (same antibody, dilution & exposure time) for each marker. Comparing pre- and post-freeze scans

Do tissues dry during freezing cycles?

Checking signal intensities (visually and min/max) within the whole slide. Dried areas keep unstained/weakly stained (typically on the borders of the section)

Are some IF channels affected more than others by freeze-thawing?

Comparing the change of min/max signals over the cycles. Is the signal change bigger in some channels? Comparing the signal change with DAPI change in same cells (i.e. compare PanCK signal change with DAPI signal change within tumor cells and Vimentin signal change with DAPI signal change within stromal cells)

Are phospho-antibodies particularly sensitive to freeze-thawing?

Comparing min/max signal change of pERK between cycles. Signal reduction could indicate that labile signaling epitopes are more affected by freezing than conventional markers.

Does freezing affect signal-to-noise ratio? Is the change bigger with some channels?

Checking background signal (min/max) for each marker. Comparing pre- and post-freeze scans

Does the possible increase in background signal result from the channel itself or from the selected marker in that channel?

Firstly, checking whether the signal-to-noise ratio of that channel is increased in previous tCycIF projects compared to other channels. As an additional confirmation, checking the signal-to-noise ratio of the selected marker in previous tCycIF projects.

POSSIBLE EFFECTS OF FREEZING:

increase of background signal

decrease of IF-signal (epitopes damage/binding of antibodies weaken)

tissue detachment

tissue cracking

drying of sections (lead to loss of signal)

nuclear shrinkage or swelling

SAMPLES:

3 different samples > samples S1, S2, S3

Different in sizes: small, medium, and big to test the integrity

3 sequential sections from each of three samples (= a total of 9 slides) > sections S1a, S1b, S1c, S2a, S2b, S2c, S3a, S3b, S3c

MARKERS:

DAPI in BV421 🡪 nuclei

Iba1_1_3 in 488 🡪 macrophages

PanCK_1_1 in 555 🡪 tumor cells

pERK_1_1 in 647 🡪 ERK/MAPK-pathway active nuclei

Vimentin_9_5 in 750 🡪 stroma

In each cycle, the same antibody dilution and exposure time should be used to be able to compare the changes in IF signals.

TIMELINE:

Week 0 (all slides):

deparaffinization, HIER, pre-staining, bleaching

staining, scanning, bleaching

freezing

Week 2 (S1a, S2a):

thawing (= 2 weeks in freezer)

staining, scanning, bleaching

Week 4 (S1b, S2b):

thawing (= 1 month in freezer)

staining, scanning, bleaching

Week 8 (S1c, S2c):

thawing (=2 months in freezer)

staining, scanning, bleaching

FREEZING PROTOCOL:

Based on 10x Genomics protocol for pre-xenium runs:

to prevent drying of the section, cryoprotectant is needed

10x promise 2 months for Xenium v1 slides in -20°C

Prepare cryoprotectant: 30% glycerol solution in PBS (RNase- and nuclease-free)

! Ensure glycerol is diluted in PBS not water

! Ensure media is thoroughly mixed

Remove all PBS from the cassette well

Add 1,000 μl 70% ethanol and incubate for 2 min at RT

Add 1,000 μl 100% ethanol and incubate for 2 min at RT (1st incubation)

Add 1,000 μl 100% ethanol and incubate for 2 min at RT (2nd incubation)

Place slides in a slide mailer containing sufficient cryoprotectant to fully submerge the slide.

Store slides in cryoprotectant at -20°C

THAWING PROTOCOL:

Based on Pre-xenium protocol for Xenium slides stored at -20°C:

Equilibrate slide mailer to room temperature

! It is important to thaw completely to ensure no ice crystals remain during slide removal as these could scratch the slide

Remove glycerol and rinse slide mailer 3x with PBS (10 ml or volume sufficient to submerge slides)

Remove slide from mailer

ADDITIONAL NOTES (found from different places, some from chatGPT):

add enough cryoprotectant to cover whole slide

incubate cryoprot. 5-10 RT before freezing (diffusion into the tissue)

be sure that no air bubbles are on top of the slide

seal the slide mailer with a cap+parafilm

keep slides on horizontal position, do not move during freezing

open the sealing around the miler after completely thawed (10-15 min)

LINKS AND REFERENCES:

10X GENOMICS GUIDE FOR XENIUM SLIDES:

Cryoprotection of slide before xenium slide (includes composition of cryoprotectant and habit for thawing:

https://kb.10xgenomics.com/s/article/13181091903373-How-can-Xenium-slides-be-stored-prior-to-a-Xenium-Analyzer-run#:~:text=Ensure%20that%20the%20slide%20is%20stored%20in,or%20slide%20seal%20applied%20to%20prevent%20evaporation.
